texto: Technical knowledge, divorced from what it is supposed to be knowledge of, yields only the illusion of understanding. It's like being able to reel off the locations in a baseball field -- first base, second base, third base, home plate, left field, right field, center field, pitcher's mound -- without having the slightest clue as to how they function in a game. You can talk the talk, but you can't walk the walk.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stanley Fish: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Technical knowledge, divorced from what it is supposed to be knowledge of, yields only the illusi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, writing-craft, field pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: Belief and knowledge are considered to be two different things. But they are not.
explicacao: Citação de Stanley Fish sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “Belief and knowledge are considered to be two different things. But they are not.” articula sabedoria, belief, two em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como two e different.

texto: the focus one finds in the grammar books is on the wrong forms, on forms detached from the underlying (or overarching) form that must be in place before any technical terms can be meaningful or alive
explicacao: Orientação direta de Stanley Fish sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “the focus one finds in the grammar books is on the wrong forms, on forms detached from the underl…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, writing-craft, forms, útil em momentos de reflexao.
